format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# Dictionaries — Key-Value Pairs for Structured Data' with no frontmatter. reading.meta.json exists and parses cleanly with all required fields (word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections). Exactly 6 fixed sections present in correct order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length_budget.notes: 1311 words within target [1200, 1600]. At 200 wpm = 6.55 min (7 min ceiling); at 230 wpm = 5.7 min (~6 min). Both estimates within [6, 8] min target.

[2] W3Schools cited in Key Concepts Add/Modify, Worked Example, In Practice Config. [3] Python docs cited in Key Concepts Checking/iterating, In Practice API response. IndexError: not used in the reading at all (corpus soft finding resolved). No nested dicts, no LLM/RAG topology, no advanced Python patterns introduced.

API acronym expansion included on first use — corpus soft finding resolved. Paragraphs ≤4 sentences throughout. Bullet list used for 4-term vocabulary block in Key Concepts. Code blocks appear inline at each concept introduction. Diagram embedded correctly in Key Concepts with an explanatory italic caption. In Practice: API named-and-deferred with expansion; no nested dicts; no advanced iteration patterns. Zero readability, formatting, or scope findings.

corpus_faithfulness.notes: 10 assertions sampled across all five content sections. Overview faithfully condenses corpus §4. Key Concepts condenses §5.1–5.7 with no new claims — the 4-term vocabulary block matches §5.1 exactly; .get() default/None matches §5.5; three iteration patterns match §5.6. Worked Example student record matches corpus §6 step-by-step (create → add → update → .get() → .items() loop); list-to-dict pattern matches corpus §6 second subsection verbatim. In Practice config dict matches corpus §7 Pattern 2; API response dict matches Pattern 3. Key Takeaways condenses corpus §10 covering all 6 LOs. No new unsupported claims detected.

verdict: PASS. All 5 checks clean. Zero findings. The reading is well-structured, within word budget (1311 words / ~6–7 min), cites all three resources throughout, introduces no forward references, resolves both corpus soft findings (API spelled out; IndexError not used), embeds the diagram correctly in Key Concepts, and faithfully condenses the corpus. The reading is ready for /generate-quiz and then HITL #3.
